search_results.title: Machine Learning for Quantum Mechanical Properties of Atoms in Molecules
search_results.snippet: Predicting atom-level quantum mechanical properties is hard because effects at very small scale dominate. Machine learning provides scalable prediction approaches for atomic properties.

search_results.title: Predicting many properties of a quantum system from very few measurements | Nature Physics
search_results.snippet: Quantum systems require predicting many properties from very few measurements. Classical shadow techniques enable scalable atom-level property prediction.

search_results.title: Introduction to The Strange World of Quantum Mechanics
search_results.snippet: Predictions of quantum mechanics have been verified experimentally to extraordinary accuracy. The atomic and subatomic, very small scales, require quantum prediction frameworks.

search_results.title: Beyond the Talent Gap: Solving the Convergence Bottleneck in AI Hardware
search_results.snippet: The Convergence Bottleneck is a fundamental problem where even with the world's best specialists, the ability to bring complex domains together is fractured. Resolution requires atomic-level coordination across blocker domains.

search_results.title: Bringing Atomistic Deep Learning to Prime Time
search_results.snippet: In atomistic deep learning research, open science and integration between academic groups and HPC centers can alleviate blockers. Resolution of atom-level problems requires coordinated infrastructure.

search_results.title: Breaking through AI data bottlenecks | InfoWorld
search_results.snippet: Breaking through AI data bottlenecks requires thinking about resolution as an atom-level problem: each blocker has a corresponding atom of fix work.

search_results.title: Prediction method of predicting a future state of a system
search_results.snippet: A method for embedding time series data within a reconstructed phase space to predict at least one future state of a system based on the rate of separation of trajectories.

search_results.title: Decoding predicted future states from the brain's physics engine - PMC
search_results.snippet: The physics network carries abstract information about predicted future contact events and contains a generative model of the physical world conducting forward simulations, serving as the brain's physics engine.

search_results.title: Statistical prediction of the future impairs episodic encoding of the present | PNAS
search_results.snippet: The brain prioritizes prediction over encoding when faced with the conflict between remembering specific past experiences and preparing for future situations where prediction mechanism removes uncertainty.

search_results.title: Prediction Market | Mechanism Institute
search_results.snippet: Prediction markets allow participants to trade outcome shares whose value depends on the resolution of specified future events. The incentive model aggregates private information into public price signals.

search_results.title: Self-Resolving Prediction Markets for Unverifiable Outcomes
search_results.snippet: Prediction market resolution mechanisms determine how outcomes are verified and settled. Self-resolving markets work even when outcomes are not directly observable; therefore market signal aligns with resolution.

search_results.title: Beyond Polymarket and Kalshi: 5 prediction markets we are paying attention to
search_results.snippet: Prediction markets turn belief into price by asking what people are willing to stake on an outcome, producing a market-driven signal that changes more regularly and perhaps more honestly than polls.
